question_sentence: What was the cause of the long lines at some US airports this week?
evidence: Security lines stretched for hours at some US airports due to a lack of TSA staff during the partial government shutdown. Many TSA agents didn’t show up to work or quit amid their first week without full pay.

question_sentence: Which country was plunged into a nationwide blackout this week?
evidence: Cuba struggled to restore power to millions of people after a nationwide blackout. The country's power grid collapsed on Monday, weeks after a US blockade of oil effectively shut off supplies.

question_sentence: Which type of weather is gripping much of the western US?
evidence: A potentially historic heat wave is set to grip much of the western US in the coming days, with some areas at risk of breaking all-time April temperature records before the month even begins.

question_sentence: Which Nordic nation was named the world's happiest country for the ninth year in a row?
evidence: Finland was named the happiest country in the world for a record ninth time in a row — with Iceland coming in at No. 2 and Denmark ranking third, according to the latest edition of the World Happiness Report.

question_sentence: An E. coli outbreak that sickened people in three states has been traced to which food?
evidence: The FDA has urged Raw Farm to voluntarily recall its raw milk cheese after identifying it as a "likely source" of an E. coli outbreak, but the company declined.

question_sentence: Which US agency said this week it could run out of cash within a year without help from Congress?
evidence: The US Postal Service chief said the 250-year-old agency could run out of cash within a year unless Congress allows it to borrow more money and charge more for postage.

question_sentence: Who is the White House chief of staff?
evidence: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles is balancing one of the most powerful jobs in the world with a recent cancer diagnosis — and does not plan to take time away, people familiar with her decision said.

question_sentence: Which rare phenomenon was spotted over the eastern US this week?
evidence: A rare fireball bright enough to be seen during the day dazzled skies and triggered a sonic boom in parts of the eastern US on Tuesday.

question_sentence: The 98th Academy Awards recently celebrated the year's biggest films and top talents. Which film won best picture?
evidence: Paul Thomas Anderson's "One Battle After Another" won best picture along with five other awards, including best director and best adapted screenplay.

question_sentence: Which US city dyes its river green each year in honor of St. Patrick’s Day?
evidence: The city of Chicago dyes its famous river bright green each March.
